config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically take priority over individual rights, because once you make that the default, it becomes too easy for governments or majorities to justify abuse in the name of the common good. In my view, society is strongest when it protects basic rights first, while still allowing reasonable limits when there is a clear and serious public interest. Coming from Indonesia, I also value community, but I have seen how “for society” can be used too loosely to silence people who are already vulnerable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika pemerintah atau mayoritas diberi terlalu banyak ruang atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” hak minoritas dan kebebasan sipil sering menjadi korban. Bagi saya, masyarakat memang perlu batasan tertentu demi keamanan dan ketertiban, tetapi hak dasar individu harus menjadi garis awal, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikorbankan. Jika tidak, istilah “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu gampang dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society's interests automatically override individual rights, because that is exactly how abuse gets normalized. In Indonesia, people often appeal to harmony, order, or "the public interest," but in practice those phrases can hide pressure on minorities, workers, or anyone who is inconvenient to those in power. Society does matter, and I am not saying individual freedom should be absolute, but the burden should be on society to prove a real necessity before limiting rights. If we reverse that default, the people with the least power usually pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai default. Dari pengalaman Amerika, saya melihat bahwa ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dijadikan alasan utama, hasilnya sering justru memperluas kekuasaan negara dan menekan kelompok minoritas atau suara yang tidak populer. Saya setuju bahwa ada situasi tertentu—seperti keselamatan publik atau keadaan darurat nyata—di mana pembatasan hak bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan alasan kabur yang mudah disalahgunakan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling kuat melindungi hak dasar sambil tetap menjaga ketertiban.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over individual rights, because that default is too dangerous and too easy to abuse. From my Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of harmony, order, and “the common good,” but I have also seen how those words can be used to pressure minorities or silence people who are inconvenient to those in power. Society does matter, and there are real cases where rights must be limited for safety or public necessity, but those limits should be narrow, justified, and accountable. If we start by assuming society comes first, the people with the least power will usually be the ones who lose.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak tegas gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diutamakan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip awal. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk memperluas kekuasaan negara dan menekan minoritas, terutama ketika batasnya tidak jelas dan tidak bisa diuji. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus benar-benar sempit, perlu, dan dapat dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan default. Masyarakat yang sehat menurut saya bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan orang per orang, melainkan yang paling disiplin melindungi kebebasan dasar sambil menangani kebutuhan publik secara terbatas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
